publicEvidence: Direct official site returned HTTP 200 over HTTPS
A collision visitor likely needs a fast estimate/intake path; exact form and hours require owner confirmation
selectedLeads.contactRoute: official_site_contact_path_to_verify; do not use until exact route is manually confirmed
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: Collision traffic is time-sensitive, but the first-page pass does not yet establish a single owner-approved estimate/intake path that can be safely featured or tracked.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Audit and simplify one collision-estimate path for mobile, separating urgent accident intake from general body-shop information after confirming the actual workflow.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: It is a narrow, practical conversion repair around a high-intent service rather than a generic redesign.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup: collision intake steps and approved shop imagery were not verified; a generic accident page would fail the quality bar.

draftCopy.contactFormScript: Hi Minor Wreck Express team — I found your live Des Moines collision-repair site while checking local service paths. I would not suggest a generic redesign; the useful question is whether a visitor with a fresh accident can reach the right estimate/intake step quickly on mobile. I can outline a no-obligation cleanup after confirming the workflow. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: Direct official site returned HTTP 200 over HTTPS
Business presents a specialist axle/wheel service category
Specialist commercial intent suggests a focused service page may be more useful than broad homepage traffic
selectedLeads.contactRoute: official_site contact/phone route to verify before any outreach
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A specialist service can lose high-intent searchers when commercial axle/wheel intent is not isolated into one clear page and measurable action.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Create one service-specific landing page for the highest-margin verified axle/wheel job, with one call/request action and tracking-ready copy.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The pitch can be tied to one service lane and lead quality, not unsupported traffic or revenue promises.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup: the priority service and real shop/vehicle assets need confirmation before a service-specific concept is credible.

draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing Des Moines commercial auto-service pages and found Merrill Axle & Wheel. Rather than pitch a full redesign, I had a narrower idea: one focused page for the axle/wheel service you most want more qualified inquiries for, with a clearer mobile call path. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: Direct official site returned HTTP 200 over HTTPS

selectedLeads.contactRoute: official site phone/form route to verify
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A multi-service PHC site can make a seasonal searcher choose between broad navigation paths before reaching the exact repair or replacement conversation.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Test one owner-selected seasonal repair page with a single mobile action and measurable source-specific CTA.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The work is an incremental page experiment that can preserve the existing site and be validated with their actual service priority.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup: exact seasonal priority, offer, and response expectation were not verified; a generic HVAC hero would be low quality.

draftCopy.contactFormScript: Hi Dalton team — I came across your live plumbing/heating/cooling site during a Des Moines service-path review. Because the site already covers several services, I would not lead with a rebuild. A tighter idea may be one seasonal repair page with a single mobile action and cleaner measurement, using only the offer and response process you confirm. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.contactRoute: official site phone/contact route to verify
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A local service business can lose map-driven visitors when profile services, hours, photos, and the fastest call route are not kept consistent with the owned site.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Run a profile/site consistency audit and update only owner-confirmed services, hours, photos, and primary call routing.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: It is a low-risk local visibility cleanup with clear deliverables and no ranking guarantee.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup: GMB fields and real photographic assets were not verified; a visual concept would not improve the evidence.

publicEvidence: Same business and official site were found through a second public listing route

selectedLeads.contactRoute: phone/site route only; do not submit any form
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A profile can send accident-search visitors to a general site without making the preferred estimate/contact route obvious; the exact profile fields need a manual audit before action.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Separate profile cleanup from a collision-estimate landing page, then use the owner-approved primary phone/form route consistently.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: This is a contained audit and routing fix, but it is lower priority until the exact profile discrepancy is verified.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup: this record is an audit queue item, not a distinct prospect; creating a page would duplicate the first selected lead.

qualityGateSummary: No new mockup created. The two strongest visual candidates require owner-approved service priorities, contact workflows, and real assets before a non-generic concept can pass QA. The fifth record is intentionally an internal duplicate audit queue item and should not receive separate outreach.
